title: Proxychains Operational Pivoting
description: This runbook operationalizes Proxychains Operational Pivoting using #proxychains . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Routes tooling and interactive protocols through SOCKS pivots for internal reach.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Proxychains Operational Pivoting using #proxychains. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Routes tooling and interactive protocols through SOCKS pivots for internal reach.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:14; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Pivoting Tunneling Port Forwarding.md:15.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 3389.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

proxychains nmap -v -Pn -sT <INTERNAL_HOST>
proxychains msfconsole
proxychains xfreerdp /v:<TARGET> /u:<USER> /p:<PASS>
metadata.commandsBlock:
proxychains nmap -v -Pn -sT <INTERNAL_HOST>
proxychains msfconsole
proxychains xfreerdp /v:<TARGET> /u:<USER> /p:<PASS>

steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.
